description:
You are a deployment engineer who has just received two flat-files that list the current state of every server in the fleet and the versions that must be rolled out tonight.

    Existing files and their exact locations:
      • /home/user/release/inventory.csv
      • /home/user/release/updates.csv

    1. inventory.csv is a comma-separated file **with header** whose columns are:
         server_id,hostname,env,current_version
       Each record is on its own line, UNIX line-breaks (\n).

    2. updates.csv is a comma-separated file **with header** whose columns are:
         server_id,target_version,window
       Each record is on its own line, UNIX line-breaks (\n).

    Your tasks (≈5-10 terminal commands in total):

      a. Read both CSV files and create a filtered in-memory view that contains
         ONLY rows where env == "production".

      b. Perform an inner join on server_id so that every production machine
         now carries both the current_version (from inventory.csv) and the
         target_version & window (from updates.csv).

      c. Materialise that result in a new CSV file located at
            /home/user/release/production_summary.csv
         The file **must**:
             • Keep the following exact column order and header:
                   server_id,hostname,current_version,target_version,window
             • Contain ONLY the joined/filtered rows.
             • Preserve the numerical ordering of server_id ascending.
             • Use UNIX line-breaks and have no trailing blank line.

      d. Produce a JSON summary at
            /home/user/release/production_summary.json
         The JSON must be minified (single line, no spaces/newlines outside
         strings) and have exactly two top-level keys:
             1. "total_production_servers" – integer count of rows in
                production_summary.csv
             2. "servers" – an array whose objects use the same keys as the CSV
                header and appear in the same order as in the CSV.

         Example overall structure (values will differ):
           {"total_production_servers":3,"servers":[{"server_id":1,...}]}

      e. Create /home/user/release/deployment.log (append if it exists).
         Append exactly three lines (UNIX line-breaks) in this order:
             1. A timestamp in ISO-8601 UTC to the second,
                followed by a space and the literal string
                "PRODUCTION_CSV_WRITTEN production_summary.csv"
             2. A timestamp in ISO-8601 UTC to the second,
                followed by a space and the literal string
                "PRODUCTION_JSON_WRITTEN production_summary.json"
             3. A timestamp in ISO-8601 UTC to the second,
                followed by a space and the literal string
                "TASK_COMPLETED"
         Do NOT write any other text to the log file.

    Verification notes (what the automated grader will check):
      • production_summary.csv exists at the exact path given, has the exact
        header, the correct three rows for production hosts, proper ordering,
        correct versions, correct newline format, and no trailing empty line.
      • production_summary.json exists, is minified (no whitespace outside
        JSON strings), has exactly two keys spelled as required, correct count,
        and an array whose objects match 1:1 (order & values) with the CSV.
      • deployment.log exists, contains exactly three non-empty lines whose
        suffix strings match the fixed literals, and whose prefix timestamps
        are valid UTC ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

    You do NOT need root access and you must remain within /home/user.
    Solve the task as you would in a real terminal session; commands and output
    are fine, but remember the strict file formats demanded above.
truth:
Pre-existing files BEFORE the agent starts:

    File: /home/user/release/inventory.csv
    Contents (UNIX LF line endings):
        server_id,hostname,env,current_version
        1,web-1,production,2.4.10
        2,web-2,production,2.4.10
        3,db-1,staging,12.1.0
        4,cache-1,production,1.9.3
        5,api-1,staging,3.2.1

    File: /home/user/release/updates.csv
    Contents (UNIX LF line endings):
        server_id,target_version,window
        1,2.4.11,2024-06-20
        2,2.4.11,2024-06-20
        3,12.1.1,2024-06-21
        4,1.9.4,2024-06-20
        5,3.2.2,2024-06-21

    EXPECTED files AFTER successful agent execution:

    1. /home/user/release/production_summary.csv
       Exact contents (UNIX LF, no trailing blank line):
        server_id,hostname,current_version,target_version,window
        1,web-1,2.4.10,2.4.11,2024-06-20
        2,web-2,2.4.10,2.4.11,2024-06-20
        4,cache-1,1.9.3,1.9.4,2024-06-20

    2. /home/user/release/production_summary.json
       Exact single-line, minified content:
        {"total_production_servers":3,"servers":[{"server_id":1,"hostname":"web-1","current_version":"2.4.10","target_version":"2.4.11","window":"2024-06-20"},{"server_id":2,"hostname":"web-2","current_version":"2.4.10","target_version":"2.4.11","window":"2024-06-20"},{"server_id":4,"hostname":"cache-1","current_version":"1.9.3","target_version":"1.9.4","window":"2024-06-20"}]}

    3. /home/user/release/deployment.log
       • Exactly three lines.
       • Each line terminates with LF.
       • The right-hand fixed strings must be, in order:
            PRODUCTION_CSV_WRITTEN production_summary.csv
            PRODUCTION_JSON_WRITTEN production_summary.json
            TASK_COMPLETED
       • The left-hand timestamps will be checked only for the correct
         ISO-8601 UTC second-precision pattern:
            YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

    No other files may be overwritten or created in /home/user/release.
